payload.reason: Plan review v1 (Claude subagent + codex, both APPROVE-with-findings): (1) scope incomplete — moving README 'does NOT ship yet' section leaves 3 dangling prose references (docs/getting-started.md, docs/adapters/claude-code.md, docs/adapters/plain.md); add the 2 adapter docs to scope + repoint. (2) stale plain-mode claim — 'super-harness plan ready' + 'review approve' verbs actually ship, so limitations.md must not propagate the stale caveat. (3) concepts.md shows only happy path — label + add rejection/abandon branches. (4) Superpowers not-shipped item dropped = intentional correction, document in PR body. (5) 'four commands' miscount. Revising to v2.

Plan v2 resolves all v1 findings (2-actor: Claude subagent + codex): (1) scope +docs/adapters/{claude-code,plain}.md (→11 files) + new Task 8 repoints 3 dangling README-section refs to docs/limitations.md; (2) stale plain-mode claim dropped — limitations.md removes the bullet + fixes FAQ, concepts notes 'plan ready', claude-code.md destaled (review approve verb ships); (3) concepts.md labels happy path + adds PLAN_REJECTED/CODE_REVIEW_REJECTED/ABANDONED branches; (4) Superpowers/plain-mode drops documented as corrections in Task 4 migration note for PR body; (5) 'four commands' miscount fixed. Deeper plain.md shipped-surface staleness deferred to OPEN-ITEMS (pre-existing). Renumber verified clean (T1-9).

Code review v1 (Claude subagent + codex). Provably-false stale claims to fix (verb ships fully per src/super_harness/cli/plan.py + review.py): (1) getting-started.md Note block 'Plain-mode plan_ready still has no public CLI emitter' — false, 'super-harness plan ready' ships; (2) concepts.md happy-path uses CLI verb 'done' not event name — fix to 'implementation_complete, emitted by done'; (3) plain.md v0.1-caveat block claims 'plan ready' doesn't ship / is v2 intended + 'requires framework adapter or EventWriter' — all false now (plan ready + review approve are public verbs). DEFERRED (not fixed here): Codex agent-adapter framing (limitations.md/overview.md/claude-code.md say Codex v0.2+) — CodexAdapter is registered but codex exec does not run PreToolUse hooks; the 'not-advertised-in-v0.1' framing is a consistent deliberate product positioning, faithfully migrated; reconciliation logged to OPEN-ITEMS. REJECTED findings: plan-doc historical 'does NOT ship yet' strings (legit migration artifact); README double pipx install (intentional self-contained Quickstart).

findings.note: Two-actor code review (Claude subagent + codex exec --sandbox read-only), v1 then v2. v1 both APPROVE-with-findings; three provably-false stale claims fixed in v2 and re-verified against source: (1) docs/getting-started.md Note block no longer says plain-mode plan_ready has no public emitter — super-harness plan ready ships (src/super_harness/cli/plan.py); (2) docs/concepts.md happy-path now names the event implementation_complete instead of the CLI verb done (matches docs/state-machine.md); (3) docs/adapters/plain.md v0.1-caveat block rewritten to reflect the shipped plan ready + review approve verbs. Gates green (doc check 0, decision check 0), all relative links resolve, no dangling README-section refs in live docs, doc-refs clean, quickstart smoke reproduced the gate block, suite 1628.

DEFERRED (logged to private/OPEN-ITEMS.md, not fixed here): codex reviewer flagged the agent-adapter surface as stale because CodexAdapter is registered/installable, while docs frame Codex as v0.2+. This is a deliberate product-positioning underclaim (codex exec does not run PreToolUse hooks, so the hot-path gate is only partially effective under Codex), faithfully migrated rather than flipped inside a docs-IA cut. Claude reviewer did not flag it. Needs a maintainer decision on a single consistent framing. Not blocking this cut.

REJECTED with rationale: (a) the committed plan doc still contains the literal string "does NOT ship yet" describing the migration — a legitimate historical artifact, and the acceptance grep scopes live docs (excludes docs/plans/); (b) README shows pipx install in both Install and Quickstart — intentional, keeps the Quickstart a self-contained copy-paste.
